sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.35 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: Is every finitely presented soluble group nilpotent-by-nilpotent-by-finite? No: for a prime$p$the group$G= a,b,c,d b^a =b^p,\;\, c^a=c^p,\;\, d^a=d,\;\, c^b=c, d^c=dd^b,\;\,[d,d^b]=1,\;\, d^p=1$is soluble of derived length~3, but is not metanilpotent-by-finite. (V.\,V.\,Bludov, Letter of 27 April 2007.)

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
